requirements.evidence: ios/LaneShadow/Views/Atoms/LSButtonStyle.swift:50-58 injects `tokens.foreground` into the button environment, and ios/LaneShadow/Views/Atoms/LSButton.swift:97-124 applies that resolved color directly to `Text` without using shared hard-coded text helpers.

Inferred mapping: tokens/platforms/swift/Sources/LaneShadowTheme/Resources/semantic.tokens.json:136-148 defines `accent.pressed = #9E4A22`, which matches .spec/prds/v2/concepts/uc-atm-02-button.html:831; ios/LaneShadow/Views/Atoms/LSButtonStyle.swift:149-156 maps primary pressed state to that token.

ios/LaneShadow/Views/Atoms/LSButtonStyle.swift:186-238 returns explicit per-variant disabled background/foreground/border values with `opacity: 1`, and ios/LaneShadow/Views/Atoms/LSButton.swift:67-85 both calls `.disabled(isDisabled)` and guards the action in `dispatch`.

ios/LaneShadow/Views/Atoms/LSButton.swift:133-143 does not resolve the icon slot through `LSIcon`: `.plus` and `.sparkle` are redrawn with custom `Canvas` views, and every other icon name is rendered as `LSIcon(...).hidden()`. The outline chip test at ios/LaneShadowTests/Atoms/LSButtonTests.swift:107-120 only checks helper values and `XCTAssertNotNil`, so this contract breach is not covered.
requirements.remediation: Route button icons through `LSIcon` with a token-driven color path, remove the hidden fallback, and add a render-level test that verifies the outline chip shows the icon at `theme.iconSize.small` with `theme.space.sm` spacing.

requirements.evidence: ios/LaneShadow/Sandbox/Stories/LSButtonStories.swift:6-31 registers all six `atoms.button.{variant}` stories with `tier: .atom` and argTypes for `title`, `isDisabled`, and `leadingIcon`; ios/LaneShadow/Sandbox/LaneShadowStories.swift:8-49 appends `LSButtonStories.all`. Direct grep gate returned OK.

tokens/platforms/swift/Sources/LaneShadowTheme/Resources/semantic.tokens.json:1378-1385 and 1490-1496 set both `control.minHeight` and `touchTarget.minTouchTarget` to 44; ios/LaneShadow/Views/Atoms/LSButtonStyle.swift:95-100 uses those values, and ios/LaneShadowTests/Atoms/LSButtonTests.swift:123-130 asserts >=44.

ios/LaneShadow/Views/Atoms/LSButton.swift:66-85 uses a single SwiftUI `Button` action that calls `dispatch` once, and ios/LaneShadowTests/Atoms/LSButtonTests.swift:133-140 verifies the dispatch path increments once.
Direct grep gate `! grep -REn 'Color\(|Color\.(red|blue|green|black|white|gray|orange|yellow|purple|pink)|#[0-9a-fA-F]{6}' ios/LaneShadow/Views/Atoms/LSButton.swift` returned OK.
Direct grep gate `! grep -REn 'Image\(systemName:' ios/LaneShadow/Views/Atoms/LSButton.swift` returned OK.

`git diff --name-only main...HEAD` contains only the 7 scoped files. ios/project.yml:127-135 adds `LSButtonStories.swift` and excludes legacy `Button.swift`; ios/LaneShadow.xcodeproj/project.pbxproj:83-87,219,520 contains the matching generated exception/source entries, so the project changes are necessary and aligned with XcodeGen.

findings.evidence: The public icon slot is still off-spec. `.plus` and `.sparkle` bypass `LSIcon` entirely via custom `Canvas` drawings, and all other `IconName` cases are passed to `LSIcon(...).hidden()`, so they disappear instead of rendering with the button's resolved foreground token.
findings.fix: Render button icons through `LSIcon` for all supported `IconName` values and provide a token-resolved color override path so the icon uses the same resolved foreground as the label.

findings.evidence: The current tests do not exercise the failing behavior. `test_outline_variant_with_leading_icon_renders_chip_layout` only checks token helper values plus `XCTAssertNotNil`, and `test_action_fires_exactly_once_per_press` calls `LSButton.dispatch` directly rather than pressing the view. The canonical task selector `-only-testing:LaneShadowTests/Atoms/LSButtonTests` also no-ops per the supplied evidence, so the advertised scoped validation did not execute this suite.
findings.fix: Add render-level assertions for icon visibility/layout and press behavior, and make the scoped xcodebuild test selector target the actual test bundle/class so the task's documented validation command executes real tests.

summary: NEEDS_FIXES. The generated project changes, story registration, disabled mapping, padding, and grep gates are acceptable, but the button icon pipeline is still not spec-compliant: `LSButton` does not render its icon slot through `LSIcon`, and most icon names are hidden. Current tests are too weak to catch that regression.
